title: pinguicula villosa var. ramosa
intro: Pinguicula villosa var. ramosa, also known as the "Ramosa Butterwort" or "Mexican Butterwort", is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the family Lentibulariaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Pinguicula villosa var. ramosa include treating skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as having potential anti-cancer and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
